title: Conference attendee badge + certificate suite — 510 personalized A6 badges and matching completion certificates, every headshot face-cropped, tonally matched, and dropped on a shared brand backdrop, then InDesign data-merged from one roster CSV and exported as print-ready CMYK PDFs
one_line_ask: From a 510-row attendee roster CSV and a folder of mixed-quality headshots, retouch every headshot to a uniform look (straighten, face-crop, tone-match, subject-isolate, shared brand backdrop, one Lightroom preset), then drive two desktop-authored InDesign templates (badge.indd + certificate.indd) with the roster via document_merge_data_layout — supplying the retouched headshots as additionalImageFiles — and export 510 A6 badges plus 510 matching completion certificates as print-ready CMYK 300dpi PDFs, with QA proofs rendered via document_render_layout and a typography recommendation from font_recommend.
deliverables: 510 print-ready A6 (105x148mm, CMYK, 300dpi) name badges — one per attendee — merged from the roster CSV with name + organization + role + headshot, exported as a multi-page print PDF (and per-badge PDFs for re-prints)
510 matching completion/attendance certificates (A4 landscape, CMYK, 300dpi) merged one-per-attendee from the SAME roster CSV (name + certificate number + track + date), exported as a print PDF

Every headshot uniformly auto-straightened, face-cropped to an identical portrait frame, exposure/contrast tone-matched across all 510, subject-isolated and placed on one shared solid brand-backdrop colour, and finished with one shared Lightroom look preset — delivered as the retouched headshot set fed into the merge
A vectorized (SVG) version of the supplied event logo for crisp placement on both layouts at any size
A recommended badge/certificate typography pairing (display + body) that reads cleanly at A6 and A4
QA proof renders (JPEG + PDF) of representative merged badges and certificates for client sign-off before the full 510-run
A short delivery manifest mapping every certificate number to its attendee row and output filename
